response.justification: Bogdan Szabo's career is firmly rooted in web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) with no meaningful C++ depth — the only dedicated C++ role was a 6-month junior stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015, supplemented by a bachelor thesis project. The role mandates 5–10 years of C++ on Windows; that bar is not remotely met. There is no Fixed Income, Fixed Income Derivatives, Investment Banking, or Capital Markets experience — RIDE Capital is a German retail investment/tax-optimization fintech, not a capital-markets environment. SQL is listed as a skill but with no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle proficiency, and the candidate's database work skews heavily toward MongoDB and NoSQL stacks. The gap across all three mandatory pillars (C++ depth, Windows focus, and domain expertise) makes this candidate unsuitable for the role.
strengths: Demonstrates strong software-craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) applicable to any engineering environment
Has some historical C++ and Java exposure, showing familiarity with compiled, statically typed languages beyond scripting
Financial-adjacent experience at RIDE Capital provides basic literacy with regulated, data-sensitive product development
concerns: C++ experience is negligible: one 6-month role in 2014–2015 and a thesis project do not approach the required 5–10 years
No capital markets, investment banking, fixed income, or derivatives domain experience whatsoever
No evidence of SQL Server or Oracle usage; recent database work centers on MongoDB and cloud-native stores
